Review the spreadsheet and ensure all formulas are correct and working.
Audited all 5 sheets; fixed 3 broken formulas in Assumptions; flagged structural issues (mostly hardcoded values, label conflicts) for user decision.

Fixes: Assumptions!B8 =B5*B6 (#VALUE!) → =B6*B7 = $12,340,290 (units × cost/unit). Assumptions!B15 =B13 (returned $1.4M, wrong reference for Year 20 equity return) → hardcoded 600000 to match Dashboard B11. Assumptions!B23 =1-B19 (returned 98%, computed off rent growth) → =1-B22 = 40% (correct NFP share). Verified math throughout: PMT $300,448 ✓ at 4.3%/50yr/$6.17M; Y1 NOI $477,945 ✓; DSCR 1.59x ✓; 60/40 NCF splits ✓; 24-Unit alt all ties ✓.

Formula bugs resolved. Outstanding issues raised to user: (a) Pro Forma, Dashboard, Landowner Waterfall, 24-Unit Alternative are 100% hardcoded — not driven by Assumptions; (b) 'Landowner Retained Equity' label means $1M in Assumptions!B14 but $600K on Dashboard B10; (c) Landowner Waterfall column C labeled 'cumulative' but values are incremental — column D holds true cumulatives.
Make the model better — implement Tier 1 improvements (wire everything to formulas).

Converted the entire workbook from hardcoded snapshot to a live formula-driven model. Assumptions sheet is now the single source of inputs; Pro Forma, Dashboard, Landowner Waterfall, and 24-Unit Alternative all derive from it.

Assumptions: added DERIVED block (E5:E9) with mortgage principal =B8*B27, annual DS =-PMT(B32/12,B33*12,E5)*12, GPR Y1, capital stack check; renamed B14 'Value Below Asking'; color-coded blue inputs / black formulas; applied $/% number formats. Pro Forma: rebuilt all 16 rows × 21 cols as draggable formulas; B-col pulls from Assumptions, C-U applies rent growth (1+B19), debt service constant from $E$6, distributions from $B$22/$B$23 share assumptions, mortgage amort uses CUMPRINC for paydown and balance, V-col is SUM totals. Dashboard: all 15 KPIs (B5:B19) and capital stack (B22:C26) wired to formulas. Landowner Waterfall: relabeled column C 'Period Income' (incremental), column D 'Cumulative'; renamed rows 6-9 to 'Years 2-5/6-10/11-15/16-20'; all C-col cells now =SUM over Pro Forma year ranges; D-col running cumulative; comparison block B18:B22 wired. 24-Unit Alternative: only B5 (units=24) and B8 (rent=2200) remain inputs; everything else derives from shared Assumptions; B15 uses growing-annuity closed form for 20-yr cum income. Verified end-to-end: changed rent 2150→2300, Dashboard NOI rose 477,945→511,290 and DSCR 1.59→1.70; reverted cleanly.

Model is fully live. Original numbers preserved exactly: TPC $12,340,290; Y1 NOI $477,945; DSCR 1.59x; 20-yr landowner cum income $3,362,302; total landowner return $5,362,302; 24-unit alt total $4,819,473. Tier 2 (sensitivity tables, scenario toggle, IRR, charts, break-even) and Tier 3 polish remain available on request.
Anything else we can do to improve the model?
Presented Tier 2 / Tier 3 / Tier 4 enhancement menu and recommended a combination.

Tier 2: IRR & equity multiple for landowner; sensitivity table (rent × vacancy); Base/Downside/Upside scenario toggle; break-even rent solver; opex line-item detail. Tier 3: charts (capital stack donut, NCF stacked column, cumulative return line, DSCR-by-year); Sources & Uses table; stabilized cap rate / refinance test; print layout; data validation on inputs. Tier 4: separate rent vs opex inflation; vacancy lease-up curve; MLI Select scoring proxy.
Awaiting user selection. Recommended bundle: items 1+2+3+6 (IRR + sensitivity + scenarios + charts) for BCH/lender pitch readiness.
Implement all 4 enhancement bundles — IRR/EM, sensitivity, scenario toggle, charts.
Added time-weighted return metrics, two sensitivity tables, three-state scenario toggle with dropdown, and four charts to the Dashboard. New 'Sensitivity' sheet created.

(1) IRR & EM on Landowner Waterfall: built 21-period cash flow series in B30:V30 (t=0 = land contributed -$2M + $1.4M Day 1 cash; t=1..19 = annual NCF distributions; t=20 = NCF + $600K equity return). =IRR(B30:V30) yields 22.04%; equity multiple = (Day1+CumIncome+Y20Equity)/Land = 2.68x. Surfaced both on Dashboard B20/B21 (inserted 2 rows; capital-stack rows auto-shifted). (2) Sensitivity sheet: 7×7 DSCR table (rent $2,000-$2,300 × vacancy 2-8%) with conditional formatting (red <1.10, green >=1.30) and yellow base case at E8; second 7×7 table for 20-yr Landowner Cum Income (rent × rent growth 1-4%) using closed-form growing annuity. (3) Scenario toggle on Assumptions D11:G18: dropdown at E12 (Downside/Base/Upside) drives B18/B19/B20/B21 via INDEX/MATCH. Tested cascade — Downside DSCR 1.34x/IRR 13.8%, Base 1.59x/22.0%, Upside 1.82x/30.3%. (4) Charts on Dashboard: Capital Stack donut (E1:K12), Cumulative Landowner Income line (L1:R12), Annual NCF Distributions stacked column (E13:K27), DSCR by Year + 1.10 threshold line (L13:R27). Chart data blocks added to Pro Forma rows 18-30.

Model is now decision-ready: live IRR/EM, scenario stress testing, two-way sensitivity, and visual charts. Zero formula errors across 6 sheets. Base case verified: TPC $12.34M / NOI $478K / DSCR 1.59x / IRR 22.0% / EM 2.68x. Tier 2 items remaining (break-even rent solver, opex line-item detail) and Tier 3-4 items (S&U table, cap-rate test, print layout, data validation, opex inflation, lease-up curve, MLI scoring) available on request.
